data.title: Tickets branch push fails due to missing .pre-commit-config.yaml on orphan branch
data.description:
## Bug Report

Every ticket CLI operation that writes to the tickets orphan branch triggers a pre-commit hook failure:

Warning: tickets branch push failed (exit 1): No .pre-commit-config.yaml file was found

The tickets branch is an orphan branch containing only .tickets-tracker/ data. It does not have a .pre-commit-config.yaml file because it is not a code branch. However, pre-commit runs on push and fails because it expects a config file.

## Impact
Tickets are created and stored locally but fail to push to origin. This is a non-blocking warning (local state is correct), but it means remote ticket state drifts from local.

## Expected Behavior
Ticket branch pushes should succeed without requiring .pre-commit-config.yaml on the orphan branch.

## Reproduction
Run any ticket create/transition command and observe the push warning in output.

## Possible Fixes
- Add PRE_COMMIT_ALLOW_NO_CONFIG=1 to the git push environment for the tickets branch
- Add a minimal .pre-commit-config.yaml to the tickets orphan branch
- Skip pre-commit hooks for tickets branch push (tickets branch has no code to lint)
